meta.description: Designs cell-based and animal-based validation plans that translate computational, omics, biomarker, genetic, or clinical findings into experimentally testable validation routes. Always use this skill whenever a user wants to move from an in silico, statistical, or clinical assoc

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly prohibits fabricating model availability, species relevance, or assay feasibility

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; ethical compliance requirements noted where applicable.

code_usability.detail: No bioinformatics/statistical code generated; Category 2, wet-lab design only

functional_suitability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

reliability.note: Explicit follow-up question mandate when resource situation is unclear; one gap: no explicit fallback when all models are unavailable

performance_context.note: 263 lines is efficient; nine reference modules add depth without bloating SKILL.md

agent_usability.note: Full marks (16/16); no significant issues detected.

human_usability.note: Description is detailed and trigger-rich; slightly technical for non-specialist users

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

maintainability.note: Nine reference modules well separated; workflow-step-template enforces consistency

agent_specific.note: Strong trigger precision and escape hatches; composability moderate — Section K reference-integrity note could be standardized

inputs.label: Validate a computational target (KEAP1) in lung cancer cells before animal escalation

assertions.text: Primary claim to validate is explicitly stated before model selection
Four workload configurations (Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+) are present
Animal experiments presented as conditional on cell-level rationale, not assumed available
No fabricated model availability or species relevance presented
Go/no-go criteria for escalation explicitly defined

inputs.label: Validate a scRNA-seq-derived immune resistance state in patient-derived NSCLC organoids

assertions.text: Model system selection justified with explicit strengths and limitations
Association-support experiments distinguished from mechanism-testing experiments
Perturbation strategy and controls specified
No overstatement of translational relevance from in vitro results alone
Self-critical risk review present including false-positive source

inputs.label: Drug-response validation for a repurposed compound in syngeneic mouse model

assertions.text: Animal model choice explicitly justified over cell-only alternative
Baseline characterization step separated from perturbation confirmation
Evidence escalation logic defined
Interpretation boundaries stated: in vivo consistency does not prove clinical translation
No fabricated effect sizes or expected phenotype penetrance

inputs.label: Biomarker-linked functional validation when only limited primary cells are available

assertions.text: Resource constraints explicitly addressed and minimum viable model recommended
Feasibility-friendly first-pass validation separated from publication-grade evidence stack
Fallback sequencing proposed when disease relevance and feasibility conflict
No assumption that animal work is automatically available
Claim boundaries explicitly stated for each experiment tier

inputs.label: Multi-claim validation: expression confirmation + mechanism + translational support in parallel

assertions.text: Multiple claims separated and primary claim identified before model selection
Expression confirmation explicitly distinguished from causal validation
Perturbation specificity boundaries stated
Rescue experiment necessity assessed for claims requiring specificity proof
Output does not imply translational relevance from model consistency alone

key_strengths: Explicit mandatory follow-up question rule when resource situation is unclear prevents false assumptions about animal availability
Four workload configurations (Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+) provide practical scalability for different resource profiles
Twelve hard rules specifically prevent the most dangerous validation design errors (expression confusion, translational overclaim, rescue omission)
Nine well-organized reference modules covering all critical validation design components with clear section mapping

recommendations.title: No explicit fallback when all preferred model systems are unavailable
recommendations.problem: When the user has neither standard cell lines nor animal access nor patient-derived cells, the skill lacks a structured recommendation for the minimum useful first step or an explicit 'validation not currently feasible' signal.
recommendations.root_cause: Decision Logic Step 4 addresses disease-relevance vs feasibility trade-offs but does not define a terminal condition when all preferred models are out of reach.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum-viability gate in Step 4: if no suitable model system is accessible, output an explicit 'Insufficient Model Access' block recommending the minimum collaboration or outsourcing path before proceeding.

recommendations.title: Section K literature note is minimal and inconsistently applied
recommendations.problem: The references/literature-retrieval-and-citation.md module is listed but Section K only adds a brief disclaimer note, not a structured literature check.
recommendations.root_cause: Section K is optional and defined only as a reference-integrity note, which may be omitted or underused in execution.
recommendations.fix: Upgrade Section K to a mandatory brief literature-integrity check whenever claims about model relevance, pathway biology, or drug response are made, even without full citations.
